title: Sovereignty
summary: Two sovereignties, opposite fates — the national kind the record says is being dissolved, and the personal kind it says must be kept.
markdown:
**Sovereignty** appears in six sessions with a split personality — and the split is the
teaching.

National sovereignty, in the record's geopolitics, is scheduled for demolition. The
directive is quoted outright in the July 2023 session: *"Directive. Destroy all border.
Destroy all sovereignty of nation. Unite humanity under one umbrella"* — and the April
2020 Morocco reading warns [Salim](/lore/salim/) that *"sovereignty of your country will
no longer exist."* Whether that unification reads as the dark agenda or as the coming
planetary civilization depends, as always in this record, on
[which timeline](/lore/positive-timeline/) the session is describing.

Personal sovereignty runs the other way: it is the thing to keep. The May 2020 session
frames refusal itself in the term — those who *"maintain their sovereignty"* against
mandates — and the practice stack ([belief](/lore/belief-protocol/), the
[Prime Directive's](/lore/prime-directive/) consent rule) presumes an individual whose
yes and no are load-bearing. The record's arithmetic is consistent: borders dissolve
upward into unity; persons do not dissolve at all.
